name: Eric Dolphy
abstract: Eric Allan Dolphy Jr. (June 20, 1928 – June 29, 1964) was an American jazz multi-instrumentalist, composer, and bandleader.

concerts.label: New Year's Eve Jazz at Carnegie Hall featuring John Coltrane, Thelonious Monk, Sonny Rollins, Nina Simone and George Crater
20th Century Innovations: Early Experiments with Jazz
20th Century Innovations: Recent Developments in Jazz

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: I got to be very close to Eric Dolphy... I got to be good friends... Eric Dolphy: groundbreaker; [a] very, very good friend
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Arturo O'Farrill
about_quotes.evidence: I know who’s going to sound like Lee Morgan, I know who’s going to sound like Eric Dolphy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Arturo O'Farrill interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 6, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: He taught a lot of people. Dexter Gordon and Eric Dolphy are the famous ones.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charles McPherson
about_quotes.evidence: Eric Dolphy and a guy named Ted Curson... were going to quit Mingus... Mingus needed a saxophone player in there... Eric Dolphy was still there... for about two weeks there were two altos and two trumpets in Mingus’ band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles McPherson interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 12, 1998 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: He’s more like in my family. He knows my three daughters, he used to come over to my house all the time... I’d go over to his house all the time, I know his mother and father
about_quotes.doc_title: Gerald Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: The 1955 Chico Hamilton Quintet created a distinctive sound with its combination of guitar, cello and woodwinds, and helped introduce Eric Dolphy to the jazz world.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 30, 2000
